scope_note: QA Quantize Algorithm cert [320]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1995) Quantum Arithmetic Book 3 (QA-3), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-08-9, Chapter 1 (Quantize). Ben's bridge from empirical measurement to the QA integer world. Core theorem: in a quantum-exact BEDA(b,e), the diagonal measure DO = (JO+KO)/2 = d*(b+a)/2 = d*d = d^2, where JO=d*b, KO=d*a, and the midpoint identity b+a=2d holds because a=b+2e => b+a=2(b+e)=2d. The Quantize inverse: given quantum-exact measurements (DO=d^2, JO=d*b), recover the integer pair by d=isqrt(DO), b=JO//d, e=d-b — exact with no floating-point rounding. CLAIM: (C1) Midpoint identity b+a=2d holds for all 81 pairs in {1..9}^2 (algebraic completeness). (C2) DO=d^2 theorem: for all 72 Cosmos pairs, (d*b + d*a)//2 == d*d exactly. (C3) Quantize reconstruction is lossless: Quantize(d^2, d*b) recovers (b,e) exactly for all 72 Cosmos pairs. (C4) Fingerprint uniqueness: all 72 Cosmos (d^2, d*b) encodings are distinct — no two Cosmos pairs share a (DO, JO) fingerprint. (C5) Satellite extension: midpoint identity and exact reconstruction hold for all 8 Satellite pairs (orbit_family=Satellite); d^2 is exact; isqrt(d^2)==d. Theorem NT: DO and JO are observer-layer projections (continuous measurements); (b,e) is the QA causal layer; the Quantize algorithm is the canonical two-crossing bridge (measurement->integer->measurement is lossless). Builds on cert [319] (QA-2 completion), cert [314] (Egyptian Ennead orbit partition, mod-9 structure).
